danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct creature designs (Mechagodzilla, King Ghidorah, Biollante, Battra, Mecha-King Ghidorah, Bagan)

danger_signals.meaning: Hostile alien forces controlling or deploying threats; persistent danger unless mothership destroyed

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental damage visualization in stage locations (Osaka, Mt. Fuji, Lake Ashino, Yokohama)
danger_signals.meaning: World-state degradation; time-sensitive threat to urban areas requiring Godzilla intervention
Damage accumulation from environmental obstacles or enemy attacks; risk of mission failure

danger_signals.visual_form: False Godzilla appearance masking mechanical interior
danger_signals.meaning: Deception threat; alien infiltration strategy; identity confusion as danger signal

safety_signals.meaning: Human military protection; mission support; rescue capability for captured scientist

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible power-up items distributed across levels
safety_signals.meaning: Power progression pathway; human scientific aid; transformation prerequisite (Super Godzilla state)

safety_signals.visual_form: Enemy elimination in side-view combat segments
safety_signals.meaning: Threat neutralization; stage progression; world-state improvement

safety_signals.visual_form: Command and control mechanism (Super X II operated)
safety_signals.meaning: Humanity's strategic coordination; Godzilla alignment with human objectives

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Japanese cities (Osaka, Mt. Fuji, Lake Ashino, Yokohama); scientific laboratory; military installations

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Control manifestation through resurrected and modified monsters (Mecha-King Ghidorah, Biollante, Bagan enhanced with serum)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mothership presence in stages; serum capsule locations as alien deployment points

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Iconic monster design; atomic dinosaur form
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Super Godzilla transformation state (enhanced power form achieved through serum collection)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Presence in Japanese locations; interaction with human-controlled Super X II

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Individual creature designs (King Ghidorah, Mechagodzilla, Biollante, Battra, Bagan)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mecha-King Ghidorah = resurrected and technologically enhanced King Ghidorah; Mechagodzilla = robot duplicate disguised as organic Godzilla
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stage-specific deployment locations; boss encounter arenas

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: UFO spaceship appearance over Osaka; King Ghidorah summoning
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Godzilla deployed by human control; military mobilization of Super X II

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Second 'Godzilla' appears at Mt. Fuji with artificial skin; revealed as Mechagodzilla
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forced combat between Godzilla and doppelgänger; confusion in human command structure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Serum capsules appear in environment; collectible power-up tokens distributed across level
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Godzilla gains strength; path toward Super Godzilla transformation unlocked

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enhanced cyborg monster manifestation; increased visual complexity vs. original King Ghidorah
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: City destruction threat meter (50% destruction threshold); urgency signal for serum collection

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Godzilla's visual form changes to powered-up appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enhanced combat capabilities; match against Mecha-King Ghidorah becomes winnable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bagan emergence from past; ancient creature revived with modern enhancement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final boss encounter; escalation of threat beyond single-timeline invasion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Godzilla returns to ocean; human transmitter disabled
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Godzilla freed from human control; serum effect wears off; return to baseline form

environmental_storytelling.example: Stages move through Japanese cities (Osaka → Mt. Fuji → Lake Ashino → Yokohama), visually depicting the spatial expansion of alien invasion threat across nation

Progressive enemy escalation from biological (King Ghidorah) to hybrid (Mechagodzilla disguise) to technologically enhanced (Mecha-King Ghidorah) to temporally displaced (Bagan) signals increasing alien sophistication
Top screen shows Godzilla's direct actions and combat; bottom screen provides strategic map context—visual metaphor for dual human-monster perspective on same conflict

environmental_storytelling.technique: Serum capsule placement as environmental puzzle
environmental_storytelling.example: Scattered Dr. Shiragami serum capsules throughout stages communicate human scientific resistance embedded in environment; collection requirement visualizes how human knowledge enables Godzilla empowerment
50% destruction threshold for Mecha-King Ghidorah stage communicates escalating stakes visually—world-state deterioration creates urgency signal through environmental damage accumulation
Optional mothership destruction in stages (prevents UFO encounters) teaches player that destroying alien command infrastructure reduces ambient threats—environmental strategy communication

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Controlled by Super X II transmitter
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Follows human direction to specific monster encounters; guided through stages toward strategic objectives
Increased combat effectiveness; capable of defeating previously matched opponents with visual dominance
Returns to ocean voluntarily; freed from human control; escapes back to natural habitat
Deploys Godzilla to threat locations; rescues captured scientist; disables control transmitter at mission end
Attacks Osaka; defeated in early stage; disappears (indicating body recovery by aliens)
Appears identical to Godzilla; forces identity verification through combat; defeated to confirm true Godzilla's superiority
Continuous harassment encounters in stages; forced combat encounters unless alien command structure eliminated
Causes city destruction; threatens 50% damage threshold; requires Super Godzilla form for victory; demonstrates alien escalation
Ultimate final boss; most challenging opponent; equals Super Godzilla in power; represents alien ability to access timeline resources

notes: Super Godzilla communicates world-state primarily through: (1) Enemy monster roster as escalating threat visualization; (2) Urban location progression as invasion map; (3) Dual-screen UI separating action (top) from strategic context (bottom); (4) Serum capsule collection as human-Godzilla cooperation mechanic; (5) Transmitter control as humanity's strategic leverage; (6) UFO mothership optional destruction as environmental threat management. The game uses monster design itself as narrative progression—biological > mechanical > hybrid > temporal—to signal alien intelligence and adaptability. Faction identity is conveyed through spatial presence (humans in cities, aliens via ships, Godzilla as mobile bridge) rather than color/sigil systems. World-state changes manifest through stage-specific threat emergence and environmental destruction metrics. The narrative arc visually communicates humanity's dependence on Godzilla, the alien invasion as multi-layered threat, and the ultimate separation (release) as narrative resolution.
